what: Maps the game's accuracy_circle_scale (characters.json) to on-screen reticle diameter in pixels, at the fixed scope-lock recording resolution (2622x1206).

relation.notes: PROPORTIONAL (offset 0), NOT linear-with-offset. accuracy_circle_scale IS the fully-bloomed reticle diameter; ~0.648 px per scale-unit, no dead zone. px/scale is uniform across classes: 48/75=0.640, 71.5/110=0.650, 162/250=0.648. Fit from 3 independent BLOOM-PEAK measurements; residuals <=0.6px. SUPERSEDES the old k=0.751,C=-25.2 (2026-07-17): that -25.2 offset was a bloom-phase artifact — the earlier AR 29px / SMG 60px points were mid-bloom snapshots, not the peak.

bloom: The reticle PULSES on the fire cadence (~20 Hz) between a contracted floor and the fully-bloomed peak. accuracy_circle_scale maps to the PEAK; a single-frame snapshot can land anywhere between floor and peak (the source of the old under-measured AR/SMG points). The AR contracted floor ~29px ≈ the boss core, which is why high Hit Rate (which suppresses bloom toward the floor) approaches all-core.

calibrationPoints.note: re-measured at the fire-cadence bloom peak; the prior 0:10 snapshot read 29px (mid-bloom / near the contracted floor).
the prior quency 60px was a mid-bloom snapshot.

crossCheck.finding: AR contracted-floor / inner-bound reading (29px) == Noir mid-band boss core (28px), measured independently. The AR ACCURACY circle (fully bloomed) is 48px, NOT 29px.
crossCheck.meaning: 29px is the AR reticle's HR-shrink floor, not its accuracy circle. Fully bloomed the AR circle is 48px => coreFracGeo(core 28, circle 48) ~= 0.34 base core fraction. As Hit Rate squeezes the bloom down toward the 29px floor (~= core), coreFracGeo -> ~1: that inner bound is where core SATURATES, which is the real 'optimal range = all-core' mechanism. This reframes the old '29px == accuracy circle => ~100% core' reading (that 29px was a mid-bloom snapshot of the accuracy circle, coincidentally equal to the floor/core).

coreDiameterVsRange.selection: Inverse-with-offset (perspective projection: apparent size ~ 1/(distance + camera offset)). Pure inverse d=k/r is RULED OUT (near d*r maxes 775 < far d*r mins 952, no overlap within range bounds). Linear d=a-b*r RULED OUT (mid-far implied range 57.7 exceeds its 35-55 bound). Inverse-offset is the only tested form with all four bands in-bounds.
coreDiameterVsRange.notes: k, c ride on fuzzy range midpoints (treat +/-10%); the FORM is robustly selected by the bounds. One hard range value at any band would pin k,c exactly.

approximationCaveat: ALL numbers here (both relations, all measured diameters, hit/miss areas) are APPROXIMATIONS that are logically self-consistent and cross-validated (e.g. AR optimal-circle == mid core to 1px), intended as a principled BASELINE. They cannot reach true in-game accuracy without the game source code (exact reticle render formula, camera params, bloom RNG). Use as a grounded starting point, refine with more measurements, never treat as measured-truth.
usage: Chain: (1) accuracy_circle_scale -> circle px via 0.648*scale (proportional, bloom peak); (2) core px -> range via 2100/coreD-47 (or range -> core px). Together they give circle-vs-core geometry at any range for AR/SMG/SG, feeding core-hit-rate and SG pellet-landing. Worked SG example: noir-sg-bands.json.
